author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Confirmed the diagnosis. `add_dep_edge` only fired its cycle
preflight when `kind == DepKind::Blocks`, but
`compute_blocked_set` propagates blocked-ness along both
`Blocks` and `ParentChild` edges. So `A blocks B` + `B
parent-of A` was a directed cycle in the *combined* blocking
graph that the single-kind walk couldn't see — and at read
time, both A and B got flagged blocked-by-each-other forever.

# Recommendation

Fixed in `0f2fb9db`. Generalized `find_blocks_cycle` to
`find_blocking_cycle` walking the union `{Blocks ∪
ParentChild}` graph. The preflight in `add_dep_edge` now
fires for `kind in {Blocks, ParentChild}`. `Related` and
`DiscoveredFrom` still skip cycle-checking — they don't
participate in blocking. Cycle envelope shape unchanged
(`Error::DependencyCycle { from, target, cycle }`); the CLI
envelope kind stays `dependency_cycle`.

Verified by:
- 5 new tests in `crates/jjf-storage/tests/integration.rs`
  (mixed-kind both orderings, pure parent-child cycle, and a
  negative for related-back-edge).
- Full workspace `cargo nextest run --workspace` green.
- QA harness `b3` now reports `[b3] NEGATIVE: mixed-kind
  cycle rejected at preflight`.

# Confidence

High. The fix matches the spec invariant the ticket
articulates ("adding any edge that creates a directed cycle
in the combined blocking graph is rejected at `add_dep_edge`
time") and the run-time semantics in `compute_blocked_set`
that define what "blocking" means.

# Open follow-ups

None blocking. The existing `dep tree` visited-set guard
already handles parent-child recursion, so this fix isn't
prerequisite to anything else.
